angle: Re-prioritizing un-built 005 levers against 029 benchmark evidence. The benchmark proved the requestQuality/citationPolicy envelope is sound and model-robust (cite-iff-good honored at 1.0), so the live weakness is the SCORE CALIBRATION the gate reads, not the envelope contract. assessRequestQuality (confidence-scoring.ts) decides good purely on a bare cosine topScore vs fixed 0.8/0.7 thresholds plus margin, with no off-corpus or semantic-relatedness check in the read path. The original buildable subset (004/026/001/003) puts zero effort on read-time calibration; every retrieval lever is parked behind the un-built 015 prod-recall gate. Re-rank and build order: (1) new calibration-discrimination phase P0; (2) 015 prod-recall gate promoted to P0 since no calibration change is provable without it; (3) 013 gap-detect P1 as the cheapest false-relevance-rate instrument; (4) 018 LLM-judge demoted P2 since it cannot reach the read-time gate. Sequence: scaffold 1 and build 015 in parallel, ship 013 observe-only immediately, flip any gate verdict only after 015 measures the recall cost.

title: Add off-corpus discrimination to the requestQuality gate (new P0 calibration phase, ahead of the original buildable subset)
problem: 029 kubernetes good@0.78 on an unrelated doc (identical across all 4 models) traces to one path. assessRequestQuality confidence-scoring.ts:471 returns good when topScore>=0.8 (TOP_DOMINANT_THRESHOLD line 78), and :476 when topScore>=0.7 (HIGH_THRESHOLD line 39) with margin or head ratio. topScore=resolveCalibrationScore(results[0]) line 453 = resolveAbsoluteRelevance, a bare normalized cosine (pipeline/types.ts:90-97). No semantic-relatedness or off-corpus check exists. tests/request-quality-aggregation.vitest.ts:61 is a committed test that ASSERTS good for a 0.78 top over a weak tail. The isotonic model (confidence-calibration-model.json, all y<=0.2) is NOT consumed by the gate (confidence-scoring.ts:388-400). Buildable subset 004/026/001/003 (spec.md:159) touches none of it; the only near lever 013 is report-only/C2-gated. Earlier 005 work fixed the OPPOSITE over-conservative bug (absolute-relevance-calibration.vitest.ts:10-14), so this over-confident direction is genuinely unaddressed.
proposal: Scaffold a new child phase adding an off-corpus signal to assessRequestQuality behind a default-OFF flag, report-only first. Options: a relative-margin floor where the top hit must beat the corpus percentile cosine not just a per-query weak tail; a query-doc lexical/anchor overlap floor gating good (kubernetes fails it); or raise the 0.8/0.7 thresholds only once 015 can measure recall cost. Emit a calibrationFlag alongside requestQuality so the rate is observable before changing the verdict.
benefit: Closes the one measured live weakness the program surfaced: stops citationPolicy citing a confident off-corpus false-relevance. Since the envelope is already model-robust (cite-iff-good 1.0), fixing the score the gate reads fixes behavior for every driver model at once.
risk: Tightening good risks re-introducing the over-conservative weak/gap collapse the absolute-relevance fix repaired. Mitigation: ship report-only first; do not change the verdict path until 015 proves aligned queries (graph/memory/routing at 0.82-0.88 good) are not regressed.

title: Promote 015 (C2 prod-mode recall gate) to P0, now the binding constraint on proving any calibration change safe
problem: The benchmark turns 015 from Tier-C unblocker into the prerequisite for the calibration fix. Any change to the gate thresholds trades false-relevance suppression against legitimate-recall loss, and there is NO instrument to measure that trade. The 015 spec confirms run-eval-v2.mjs already emits prod-lens completeRecall@3/@5/@8 but performs no baseline comparison and its exitCode 1 (line 357) is a crash handler not a recall verdict, and single-target goldens saturate. The phase-map (spec.md:160) treats 015 as one-of-five conditional items; the new evidence makes it the gating dependency for the highest-priority work.
proposal: Build 015 next, in parallel with scaffolding Proposal 1 and before flipping any verdict. Its scoped deliverables (run-spec-recall-gate.mjs PROMOTION/REGRESSION reading only the prod lens, a multi-target golden with relevance sets, a stored baseline) certify kubernetes-class suppression without dropping completeRecall on aligned queries. Add the kubernetes/authentication pair plus a small red-team class as hard_negative golden rows so the gate measures false-relevance suppression directly.
benefit: Unblocks the safe path for Proposal 1 and every Tier-C candidate at once, satisfying the program own law that no retrieval claim carries measured support until the prod-path read moves.
risk: Real cost is building multi-target goldens with curated relevance sets, subjective labeling work. Mitigation: the spec reuses harness exports (buildSearchLenses, meanCompleteRecallProfile) so no harness rewrite; seed goldens from the already-authored class-tagged 029 query set.

title: Promote 013 (B3 gap-detect) to P1 as the cheap standing instrument for the false-relevance rate
problem: 029 measured the false-relevance on ONE off-corpus term; its limitations note (029 implementation-summary.md:109) says a single term does not measure the rate, a standing red-team set would. 013 captures an aggregate impression signal at the result-assembly seam, report-only behind default-OFF SPECKIT_RETRIEVAL_GAP_DETECT; originally a routine P2 GO-on-cost item (spec.md:186). That seam is the natural home for logging a low-relative-relevance cited verdict.
proposal: Build 013 ahead of the other Tier-B/novel GO-on-cost levers and extend its report-only detector to emit a cited-but-low-relative-relevance edge. Observe-only, never changes the verdict, ships before 015. Answers the 029 red-team follow-up (029 implementation-summary.md:18).
benefit: Converts the single anecdote into a continuously-measured rate at near-zero risk (report-only, default-off), giving Proposals 1 and 2 their empirical target before and after any change. Smallest effort of the three retrieval-side moves.
risk: Low blast radius (report-only, default-off, no auto-apply). Only risk is scope creep into acting on the edges, which the spec fences as C2-gated; keep it observe-only.

title: Demote 018 (C5 LLM-judge scorer) to P2: a write-path quality input that cannot touch the read-time gate the benchmark indicted
problem: Reading improve quality scoring as the fix is wrong: 018 cannot reach the gate. Its spec feeds an LLM-judge quality_score into applyValidationSignalScoring in stage2-fusion.ts as a bounded ten-percent band on the fusion/ordering score. The requestQuality gate reads resolveAbsoluteRelevance (bare cosine, confidence-scoring.ts:453, pipeline/types.ts:90-97), NOT the fused score. A ten-percent ranking nudge cannot flip a 0.78 off-corpus cosine below 0.7. 018 is also Tier-C CONDITIONAL, blocked on the same 015 gate (benchmark-and-test-status.md:40).
proposal: Keep 018 scaffolded but explicitly behind 015 and the calibration phase. Record in the phase-map that the kubernetes finding is a read-time gate-calibration issue, not a write-time document-quality issue, so the two are not conflated in sequencing.
benefit: Prevents misallocating effort to a costly LLM-judge build under the belief it fixes the false-relevance, keeping the buildable budget on the read-time gate the benchmark indicted.
risk: 018 has independent merit as a document-quality input, so demote means re-sequence not cancel. Leave the scaffold intact; the demotion is purely ordering.
